picks.title: Reallusion Unlocks Limitless Creation: iClone Personal Debuts Alongside AI Studio Official Launch
picks.excerpt: Reallusion released iClone Personal as a permanently free tier alongside the official launch of AI Studio, establishing a zero-barrier pipeline that bridges traditional 3D artistry with next-generation AI workflows for animation and VFX.
picks.rationale: A permanently free iClone tier plus AI Studio launch hands animators a zero-cost 3D-to-AI pipeline they can adopt tomorrow.

picks.title: This is the Biggest AI Filmmaking Partnership Yet
picks.excerpt: Curious Refuge's weekly AI filmmaking roundup covers the latest generative-video tools, studio partnerships and the arrival of interactive AI movies for creators.
picks.rationale: Curious Refuge's weekly roundup keeps filmmakers current on new gen-video tools, studio deals and interactive AI movies in one watch.

picks.title: Shanghai Film Festival Takeaways: Debut Directors Rule the Golden Goblet While AI Remakes the Industry Around Them
picks.excerpt: Shanghai integrated AI more prominently into its programming than ever, with dedicated workshops and partnerships exploring generative video, image creation and audio applications, as panelists debated whether AI complements or threatens human creativity.
picks.rationale: Shanghai's expanded AI programming and workshops signal how festivals are normalizing generative tools across the filmmaking workflow.

picks.title: How Annecy is Welcoming Discussion on Industry Topics Including AI, Canal+
picks.excerpt: The Annecy animation festival is hosting Think Tank AI4Animation, a day-long session laying the foundations for a white paper on AI in animation and how generative tools will reshape animator careers and workflows.
picks.rationale: Annecy's AI4Animation think tank is drafting a white paper that could shape how generative tools enter animator careers and pipelines.

picks.title: French Animators' Group Demands Clarity on Annecy's Generative AI Policy
picks.excerpt: A French animators' group is pressing Annecy for a clear generative-AI policy, advancing '3Cs' principles (compensation, control, consent) on the use of copyrighted work to train AI as the festival becomes a flashpoint for AI in animation.
picks.rationale: The 3Cs push (compensation, control, consent) frames the consent and rights stakes every animator faces as studios adopt AI.
